title: Sixteen Identities FAIL: falsifier with wrong H value and wrong parity
_primary_source_note: Intentional falsifier for validator testing (not a new claim), same 16-identity system as the PASS fixtures (Iverson, 1988) Pyth-1 Ch V and (Wildberger, 2005) chromogeometry.
_fixture_purpose: Falsifier: direction (3,2) declares H=999 (should be 17), and direction (4,1) declares C=9 instead of 8. Both trip SI_IDEN, which compares declared values against values genuinely recomputed from (d,e) -- SI_REL and SI_PAR use the recomputed values internally so they still pass on their own terms; only the declared-vs-computed cross-check (SI_IDEN) catches these two planted defects.

directions.note: correct, kept valid so this fixture isolates the defects
FALSIFIER: C should be 8 (2*4*1=8), declared 9; breaks C=4-par rule (9 mod 4 = 1)

algebraic_relations: L = CF/12 (always integer for primitive directions)

validation_checks.description: FALSIFIER: direction[1] H declared 999, computed 17; direction[2] C declared 9, computed 8
relations hold on recomputed values regardless of declared-field defects
parity holds on recomputed values regardless of declared-field defects
